title: $:/plugins/linonetwo/tidgi-routing-chain
author: LinOnetwo
name: TidGi Routing Chain Indicator
description: (Requires next TidGi) Show why a tiddler is routed to a public or private workspace, with the highest-priority tag chain in the toolbar tooltip.
readme:
<$button class="tc-btn-invisible" style="overflow: hidden;white-space: pre;width: 100%;" message="tm-open-external-window" param="https://github.com/tiddly-gittly/tiddlywiki-plugins/tree/master/src/tidgi-routing-chain">{{$:/core/images/github}} <$text text="https://github.com/tiddly-gittly/tiddlywiki-plugins/tree/master/src/tidgi-routing-chain"/></$button>

!! TidGi Routing Chain Indicator

Shows a ViewToolbar button in [[TidGi Desktop|https://github.com/tiddly-gittly/TidGi-Desktop/]] that indicates whether the current tiddler is routed to a ''main'' or ''sub-wiki'' workspace.

Hover the button to see the '''highest-priority''' matching tag chain returned by TidGi (same rules as filesystem save routing).

!! Requirements

* '''Next TidGi Desktop release''' (or newer) that exposes `window.service.wiki.getTiddlerRoutingInfo` — this plugin will not activate on older TidGi builds
* Current wiki has at least one sub-wiki with routing settings enabled (`tagNames` / `includeTagTree` / filters)

!! Usage

# Open a tiddler in the story river
# Look at the view toolbar button (preview open/closed icons)
# Hover to inspect the winning routing chain
# Click to add/remove the configured private tag (default: `私有`)

!! Config

* `$:/plugins/linonetwo/tidgi-routing-chain/config/private-tag` — tag added/removed by the button (default `私有`)
